videos.topic: GoHighLevel vs HubSpot 2026: Which CRM Actually Saves You Money?
videos.research_summary: GoHighLevel Starter plan is $97/mo with unlimited contacts and 3 sub-accounts. GoHighLevel Unlimited is $297/mo with unlimited sub-accounts and white-labeling. HubSpot CRM is free but Marketing Hub Professional starts at $800/mo (billed annually) with a 2,000 contact limit. HubSpot Enterprise is $3,600/mo. GoHighLevel includes built-in funnel builder, email marketing, SMS, calendar booking, and CRM in every plan. HubSpot charges separately for Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, and CMS Hub. For agencies managing multiple clients, GoHighLevel's SaaS Mode ($497/mo) lets you white-label and resell, while HubSpot has no equivalent. HubSpot has stronger native reporting and a larger app marketplace (1,400+ integrations vs GoHighLevel's ~50). HubSpot is better for enterprise teams that need advanced attribution and custom objects.

For agencies managing multiple clients, GoHighLevel's SaaS Mode ($497/mo) lets you white-label and resell, while HubSpot has no equivalent. HubSpot has stronger native reporting and a larger app marketplace (1,400+ integrations vs GoHighLevel's ~50). videos.topic: GoHighLevel Pricing 2026: Every Plan Explained in 5 Minutes
videos.research_summary: GoHighLevel offers three main plans: Starter ($97/mo), Unlimited ($297/mo), and SaaS Pro ($497/mo). All plans include unlimited contacts. Starter includes 3 sub-accounts, Unlimited has unlimited sub-accounts with white-labeling, and SaaS Pro adds SaaS Mode for reselling.
videos.notebooklm_prompt: Create a YouTube video script about "GoHighLevel Pricing 2026"...

videos.research_summary:
# GoHighLevel vs Kartra: 2026 Pricing & Features

## Current Pricing (2026)

**GoHighLevel Plans:**[7]
- **Starter:** $97/month ($970/year) — 3 sub-accounts, unlimited contacts, unlimited users, 24/7 support, all core features
- **Unlimited (Pro):** $297/month ($2,970/year) — unlimited sub-accounts, user/agent reporting, rebill phone & email (no markup), basic API access
- **Agency Pro:** $497/month ($4,970/year) — SaaS mode, automated sub-account creation, rebill phone & email with markup, advanced API access
- **Enterprise:** Custom pricing for high-volume businesses[7]

**Kartra Plans:**[3][5]
- **Essentials:** $59/month ($52/month annual) — funnels, pages, forms, 500 contacts, 10,000 emails, SMS
- **Starter:** $119/month ($99/month annual) — unlimited pages, products, memberships, video hosting, calendars, Kartra AI, no transaction fees
- **Growth:** $229/month ($189/month annual) — 12,500 contacts, 3 custom domains
- **Professional:** $549/month — up to 25,000 contacts, includes affiliate management and webinars[5]

## Key Feature Differences

**GoHighLevel Strengths:**[3]
- Funnels, websites, course hosting, CRM, email and SMS marketing, automations, calendars
- Designed for agencies managing multiple clients/sub-accounts
- Flat pricing model that doesn't increase with contact volume

**Kartra Strengths:**[10]
- Funnels, email, checkout, memberships, video hosting, helpdesk, affiliates all included
- No transaction fees
- Full feature set included in all plans (you only upgrade for contact limits, not features)
- Video analytics included

## Hidden Costs & Real Pricing Comparison

**GoHighLevel requires third-party integrations with additional costs:**[4]
- Email delivery via Mailgun (usage-based costs)
- SMS/calls via Twilio (usage-based costs)
- One analysis estimates GoHighLevel can cost **2–3x more per month than Kartra** for solo business owners once integration and messaging fees are added[4]

**Kartra's all-inclusive model:**[4]
- No Twilio, Mailgun, or add-ons required
- One monthly price with no hidden fees for emails or automations

## User Statistics & Reviews

- **Kartra:** 3.9 stars out of 5 with 57 reviews on G2[5]

## Real User Case Study

One creator documented switching from Kartra to GoHighLevel, noting their Kartra bill had climbed to approximately $549/month (Professional plan with additional tools), but reduced their total software stack to $97/month with GoHighLevel by eliminating stacked subscriptions.[8]

## Known Limitations

**Kartra:**
- Contact limits increase costs as your list grows[1]
- Entry-level plans have strict contact and domain limits[5]
- Affiliate management and webinars only available in $229+ plans[5]

**GoHighLevel:**
- Requires setup of external integrations (DNS, API connections) for messaging[4]
- Usage-based SMS and email costs can accumulate unpredictably[4]
- Longer setup and maintenance time for new users[4]

## Best Fit Summary[1]

- **Kartra:** Solo creators and small teams with simple needs
- **GoHighLevel:** Agencies and automation-driven businesses managing multiple clients, where flat pricing provides predictable scaling costs

**Note:** I cannot confirm recent updates from the last 6 months as current information beyond April 2026 is not available in these search results.

- Email delivery via Mailgun (usage-based costs)

videos.research_summary:
### 1. Current Pricing (as of 2026)
GoHighLevel offers three core monthly plans with annual discounts (20% savings):  
- **Starter**: $97/month ($970/year or ~$80.83/month equivalent). For single-location businesses; includes core CRM, funnels, websites, forms, calendars, and 3 sub-accounts (some sources say no sub-accounts).[1][2][3][6]  
- **Unlimited** (also called Agency Unlimited): $297/month ($2,970/year or ~$247.50/month equivalent). Unlimited sub-accounts, white-label desktop app, API access, membership sites, advanced automations, rebilling without markup.[1][2][3][6][8]  
- **Agency Pro** (SaaS Pro mode): $497/month ($4,970/year or ~$414.17/month equivalent). Everything in Unlimited plus SaaS mode for reselling, automated sub-account creation, rebilling with markup, AI conversational bot, advanced API, priority support, custom client domains.[1][2][3][6][8]  
Additional usage-based fees apply to all plans: SMS/email/phone (via Twilio/LC Phone), AI tool credits, workflow executions beyond limits. 14-day free trial standard; some partners offer 30 days. No free plan. Enterprise is custom-priced.[1][2][6]

### 2. Key Features and Recent Updates (Last 6 Months)
**Workflow automation features** (core to all plans, advanced in Unlimited/Pro): Unlimited customizable accounts, complex automation software for repetitive tasks, API access for custom integrations (advanced in Pro).[3][4]  
- Recent updates (2025 per sources): Added AI conversational bot and AI features to higher tiers (Unlimited/Pro); workflow executions now have separate usage fees.[2][3]  
No updates specified in last 6 months (post-Oct 2025) from results; AI enhancements from 2025 carry over.[2]

### 3. Real Competitor Comparisons with Specific Pricing Differences
Limited direct competitor data in results; one source compares to SchedulingKit (exact pricing not detailed).[2]  
| Platform | Entry Plan | Mid Plan | High Plan | Key Difference |
|----------|------------|----------|-----------|---------------|
| **GoHighLevel** | $97/mo (Starter, 3 sub-accounts) | $297/mo (Unlimited) | $497/mo (Pro, SaaS) | Usage fees extra; annual 20% off.[2][3][6] |
| **SchedulingKit** | Not specified | Not specified | Not specified | Positioned as lower-cost alternative; "watch out for GHL hidden fees" implies SchedulingKit has fewer add-ons.[2] |

### 4. Known Limitations or Common Complaints from Real Users
- Steep learning curve requires significant time investment.[2]  
- Usage-based extras (SMS/calls/email/AI credits/workflows) add unpredictable costs on top of subscription.[1][2][3]  
- AI features and white-label limited to Pro ($497); Starter lacks sub-accounts/SaaS.[1][2]  
- No free plan; minimum $97/mo commitment after 14-day trial.[2]  
Sources note these as "hidden costs" but no direct user quotes; complaints inferred from pricing guides.[2][3]

### 5. Specific Statistics or Data Points with Sources
- Annual billing discount: 20% across plans (Starter ~$77/mo equiv., Unlimited ~$237/mo, Pro ~$397/mo).[3]  
- Break-even for Pro plan: 2 clients at $297/mo each covers $497 cost; 5 clients yields margins.[3]  
- Pricing history: Starter rose from $79 (2023) to $97 (2024); SaaS Pro introduced 2023 with AI in 2025.[2]  
- Trial length: Official 14 days; partner links extend to 30 days.[1][6]  
No additional stats (e.g., user growth, churn rates) in results. If needed beyond this, search results insufficient.

videos.research_summary:
# GoHighLevel for Real Estate Agents: Current Pricing and Features (2026)

## Current Pricing (2026)

GoHighLevel offers three main pricing tiers for agencies and real estate professionals[1][8]:

| Plan | Monthly | Annual | Best For |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Starter** | $97/mo | $970/yr | Solo agents, 1-3 businesses |
| **Unlimited (Agency Pro)** | $297/mo | $2,970/yr | Growing agencies, unlimited clients |
| **Agency Pro (SaaS Mode)** | $497/mo | $4,970/yr | Agencies reselling as SaaS |

Annual billing provides approximately two months free[4].

## Included Features by Plan

**Starter Plan ($97/mo):**
- CRM and pipeline management[2]
- Email marketing[2]
- Basic automations[2]
- 3 sub-accounts maximum[8]
- SMS/text capabilities[2]

**Unlimited Plan ($297/mo):**
- Everything in Starter plus[8]
- Unlimited sub-accounts[4][8]
- Unlimited contacts and users[8]
- Branded desktop app with custom domains[4][8]
- Basic API access[8]
- User/agent reporting[8]
- Rebill phone & email (no markup)[8]

**Agency Pro/SaaS Mode ($497/mo):**
- Everything in Unlimited plus[8]
- SaaS mode capabilities[8]
- Automated sub-account creation via Stripe[7]
- Advanced API access[8]
- Rebill phone & email with markup[8]
- Full white-label control[7]

## Real Estate Agent-Specific Value

For real estate professionals, GoHighLevel replaces 7-10 tools, generating $300-$1,300 in monthly savings[2]:

- **CRM replacements** (Follow Up Boss, kvCORE, LionDesk): $69-499/mo saved[2]
- **Email marketing** (Mailchimp, Constant Contact): $20-100/mo saved[2]
- **SMS/text** (Textedly, EZ Texting): $25-50/mo saved[2]
- **Landing pages** (ClickFunnels, Leadpages): $97-297/mo saved[2]

Total potential savings: $472-$1,361/mo when consolidating these tools[2].

## Niche-Specific Pricing for Real Estate

Real estate agents specifically are quoted at these retainer ranges[1]:

| Service Level | Monthly Cost |
|---|---|
| Minimum Viable | $297/mo |
| Strong Package | $497/mo |
| Premium (AI + Full Automation) | $797/mo |

## Known Limitations

Based on available information, the search results do not provide specific documented limitations or common user complaints. The Starter plan's restriction to 3 sub-accounts is a clear technical limitation[4][8], but user-reported pain points from the past 6 months are not included in the provided sources.

## Recent Updates and Features (Last 6 Months)

The search results do not contain specific information about GoHighLevel updates released in the last 6 months. The sources reference "AI + Full Automation" as premium service offerings[1], but specific recent feature releases or update dates are not documented in these materials.

## Competitor Comparisons

**The search results do not provide direct competitor pricing comparisons.** To give you verified competitor data (such as pricing for comparable CRM platforms like Follow Up Boss, kvCORE, or LionDesk), I would need sources containing that specific information, which are not included in these results.

## Promotional Information

- Free 14-day trial available on all plans[8]
- Team leads and brokers: The Unlimited plan pays for itself if you sign 1-2 agents as white-label subaccounts, with each agent charged for their tier and the broker taking a cut[2]

**Caveat:** The sources provided are primarily from agency marketing blogs and GoHighLevel's official site. For unbiased user complaints and limitations, independent review sites or user forums would provide more balanced information that I cannot verify from these sources.

videos.research_summary:
### 1. Current Pricing (as of 2026)
GoHighLevel offers three core plans, all including **reputation management** as a standard feature across Starter, Unlimited, and Agency Pro (SaaS Mode). Pricing is monthly with 20% annual discount; usage fees for SMS, email, calls, AI credits, and workflows are extra on all plans. 14-day free trial standard.[1][2][3][5]

| Plan Name | Monthly Price | Annual (per month) | Key Inclusions Relevant to Reputation Management |
|-----------|---------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| **Starter** | $97 | $77 (~$970/year) | Unlimited contacts/users, CRM, pipelines, email/SMS (usage extra), reputation management, calendar, funnels/websites, 3 sub-accounts.[1][2][3][5] |
| **Unlimited** (Most Popular) | $297 | $237 (~$2,970/year) | Everything in Starter + unlimited sub-accounts, API access, white-label desktop app, advanced automations.[1][2][3][5] |
| **Agency Pro (SaaS Mode)** | $497 | $397 (~$4,970/year) | Everything in Unlimited + SaaS mode (white-label reselling, auto sub-account creation, rebill with markup), Conversation AI, advanced API, priority support.[1][2][3][5] |

Add-ons: Online listings management $30/month per sub-account; other extras like HIPAA $297/month, premium prospecting $29/month per sub-account.[5][9]

### 2. Key Features and Recent Updates (Last 6 Months)
**Reputation management** features include review requests, monitoring, response automation, and online listings management (extra fee).[1][2][3][5] Core tools: automated review campaigns via SMS/email, Google/Yelp/Facebook integration, sentiment analysis in dashboards.

- **Recent updates (Oct 2025–Apr 2026)**: AI conversational bot enhancements added to SaaS Pro (from 2025 rollout); no specific reputation module changes noted in last 6 months—core pricing/features stable from 2025.[2][8] Workflow Pro executions now usage-based extra across plans.[1]

### 3. Real Competitor Comparisons with Specific Pricing Differences
GoHighLevel bundles **reputation management** (valued at $200+/month standalone) into all plans at no extra base cost, unlike dedicated tools.[3]

| Category | GoHighLevel (All Plans) | Competitor | Competitor Monthly Cost | Savings with GoHighLevel |
|----------|--------------------------|------------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| **Reputation Management** | Included (Birdeye/Podium equivalent) | Birdeye or Podium | $200+ | $200+/month[3] |
| Full Stack (CRM + Reputation) | $97–$497 | Pipedrive Professional + Birdeye | $49/user + $200+ = $249+ | Up to $152+/month on Starter[3] |
| Marketing Suite | $97–$497 | ClickFunnels + ActiveCampaign + Twilio + Podium | $97 + $49+ + $50+ + $200+ = $396+ | Up to $299+/month on Starter[3] |

Replaces $400–$600+/month in separate tools.[3]

### 4. Known Limitations or Common Complaints from Real Users
- **Usage fees add up**: SMS/email/calls/AI billed separately (e.g., LC Phone, LC Email); no unlimited sending—common complaint for high-volume users.[1][2]
- **Starter limits**: Only 3 sub-accounts; insufficient for agencies with 5+ clients.[3][5]
- **Steep learning curve**: Complex interface overwhelms beginners despite 24/7 support.[3]
- **No free plan**: 14-day trial only; some users report billing issues post-trial.[2]
- Not sure on 2026-specific user complaints beyond pricing stability—no fresh review aggregates in results.[1][2]

### 5. Specific Statistics or Data Points
- **Pricing stability**: Core plans unchanged 2025–2026 at $97/$297/$497; Starter rose from $79 (2023) to $97 (2024).[2][8]
- **Annual savings**: Exactly 20% discount (e.g., Starter $97 → $77/mo).[1][3]
- **ROI threshold**: Agency Pro ($497) positive at 2 paying clients via SaaS rebilling.[1]
- **Sub-accounts**: Starter max 3; Unlimited infinite.[5]
- **Trial length**: 14 days standard; some partners offer 30 days.[1]
videos.notebooklm_prompt:
Create a YouTube video script about "gohighlevel reputation management" for the channel "HighLevel AI" (highlevel.ai).

GOAL: Educate viewers about gohighlevel reputation management and naturally mention that highlevel.ai has a detailed review/comparison/guide on this topic. The call-to-action is to visit highlevel.ai for more details.

TARGET AUDIENCE: Small business owners, marketing agencies, and entrepreneurs evaluating GoHighLevel or already using it.

STYLE GUIDELINES:
- Conversational, energetic tone — like explaining to a friend
- Start with a strong hook in the first 5 seconds (question, bold claim, or surprising stat)
- Keep it under 8 minutes
- Use simple language, avoid jargon
- Include specific numbers, pricing, and real feature names (no vague claims)
- End with a clear CTA: "Check out highlevel.ai for the full breakdown"

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook (5-10 seconds) — grab attention immediately
2. Context (30 seconds) — why this topic matters right now
3. Main content (4-6 minutes) — the meat of the topic with specific details
4. Comparison or unique angle (1 minute) — what makes this different/better
5. CTA + outro (15 seconds) — visit highlevel.ai
